responses.daily_approver_name: Muhannad Allazzam - mentioned in meetings as the marketing lead. Please confirm if he should be the daily approver for this project.
responses.brand_story_mission: Ein Abaya has been building the brand for around four to five years, with heavier marketing focus over the last two years. The brand appears to sit between accessible elegance and premium quality for Saudi women, with the goal of offering pieces that feel elevated without becoming unreachable.
responses.brand_positioning: The brand is not positioned as ultra-niche only for a very high-end segment. Awatif described Ein Abaya as a brand that can be worn by a university student and also by a deputy minister, with quality and elegance as the bridge between those audiences.
responses.brand_ladder: Maison Ein was described as the upgraded line of Ein Abaya: higher quality, higher pricing, and more elevated positioning, while still benefiting from the trust and data built by the core Ein Abaya brand. Dresses are also an expansion area, not only abayas.
responses.target_audience: Broad Saudi modest-fashion audience with a premium taste level. Existing customers already trust the quality, while the new site should also help convert first-time visitors who do not know the brand yet.
responses.persona_notes: Known segments from meetings: 1) the broader Ein Abaya customer who values elegant, wearable quality at accessible-premium prices, and 2) the higher-end customer for Maison Ein or more elevated lines. Please confirm if there are other important segments we should design for.
responses.best_sellers_collections: Known collections or themes mentioned so far: AZAL, Season of Blossoms, Maison Ein, and dresses expansion. Please confirm the current priority collections, strongest sellers, and any products that must lead the new Zid store.
responses.product_priorities: The new site should better showcase the product experience, lookbook quality, reviews, lifestyle imagery, and more immersive storytelling so customers can 'live in the product' before purchasing.
responses.tone_of_voice: Elegant, polished, premium, and culturally aligned. The tone should feel refined and trustworthy rather than loud or discount-driven, while still being clear enough for direct-response selling.
responses.customer_journey_objections: From the meetings, likely objections include lack of social proof, product pages that do not fully sell the experience, sizing or quality uncertainty for new visitors, and delays in welcome-offer follow-up through WhatsApp. Customers often discover through Meta/Instagram first, then evaluate the store and product page experience before buying.
responses.current_channels_performance: Instagram / Meta appears to be the strongest channel today. TikTok is active but results are mixed. Google was tested but not maintained due to lower results than other channels. Snapchat was tested but did not perform well at the time. WhatsApp is being used in the current customer flow.
responses.growth_notes_what_worked: Lifestyle visuals and influencer-led collection presentation appear important. Results change significantly by season and collection, and there are periods within the month when performance drops and acquisition cost rises.
responses.platform_transition_context: The current store context is Salla. The project is now confirmed to start on Zid. Continuity matters: the team does not want to stop current selling momentum while the new site is being built.
responses.analytics_ads_access: Known platforms and systems mentioned in meetings: Meta / Instagram, TikTok, Google Ads (tested), WhatsApp, and store analytics. Please confirm exactly which accounts can be shared now, and whether GA4 and GTM are already in place.
responses.social_links_overview: Known active presence mentioned: Instagram, TikTok, Snapchat, and WhatsApp. Please add the exact links or handles for the accounts we should reference in the project.
responses.welcome_offer_and_promotions: A welcome offer flow exists around WhatsApp, but there was a complaint that the current automation sends too slowly and sometimes requires manual follow-up from staff. Please confirm the exact welcome offer and any evergreen promotions that should be visible on the new site.
responses.review_social_proof_assets: Reviews were explicitly mentioned as something the new website should include more clearly. The team also discussed stronger social proof, customer feedback, and visible trust elements for new visitors.
responses.extra_notes_for_launch: Website rebuild is the first priority. The new build should improve product storytelling, social proof, product-page conversion, and onboarding to offers without creating unnecessary friction for the internal marketing team.
